That gives me a factual answer rather than a guess. Here's what the project's own records (`.cx/audit-trail.jsonl`, which logs every file-touching tool call, plus the session observations) actually show — useful for your call with Gerald:

## For the map specifically: no specialist fan-out

The map was built across **3 sessions** (including this one), and in all of them the work is attributed to a **single agent — `construct`** working directly. I found **no record of sub-agents being dispatched** during any of the map work. So the map — authoring the Hanover coordinates, `buildMapData()` with its unit tests, the Leaflet island, the pin/popup/`fitBounds` iteration, and today's OpenStreetMap re-verification + the price-tag removal — was Construct end-to-end, not a committee.
